item_content.content: Only true for a binary system of constant molar density (concentration)
• Occur at constant temperature and pressure for gas system
One mole of A move in z direction, one mole of B move in opposite direction

content.math_content: \begin{array}{l} c _ {A} + c _ {B} = c = \frac {P}{R T} \\ J _ {A} = - J _ {B} \\ - D _ {A B} \frac {d c _ {A}}{d z} = - (- D _ {B A} \frac {d c _ {B}}{d z}) \\ D _ {A B} = D _ {B A} \\ \end{array}

item_content.content: • Based upon the kinetic theory of gases
Assumption:
✓ Ideal gas
✓ Low density gaseous mixture
✓ Molecules are regarded as rigid spheres that exert no intermolecular forces
✓ Elastic collision between the rigid molecules

paragraph_content.content: Diffusivity for an ideal gas mixture of species A diffusing through its isotope A*
Substituting the expression into first equation
????: Molecular weight of diffusing species A (kg/mol)
??: Lennard-Jones diameter of the spherical molecules (m)

item_content.content: • A molecule travels in a straight line before collision
• The distance travelled by a molecule before collision is termed free path
• Mean free path: average distance travelled by a molecule between two successive collision
• Mean free path increases as the number of molecules per unit volume decreases (less collision)
When two gas molecules approach one another, they cannot come closer beyond a certain distance
• Is the closest distance between the centers of the two molecules taking part in a collision
• Is the distance at which the intermolecular potential between the two particles is zero
Related to the mean free path: smaller the collision or molecular diameter, the larger the mean free path

paragraph_content.content: A and B with unequal diameters

content.math_content: D _ {A B} = \frac {2}{3} \left(\frac {\kappa}{\pi}\right) ^ {\frac {3}{2}} N ^ {\frac {1}{2}} T ^ {\frac {3}{2}} \frac {\left(\frac {1}{2 M _ {A}} + \frac {1}{2 M _ {B}}\right) ^ {\frac {1}{2}}}{P \left(\frac {\sigma_ {A} + \sigma_ {B}}{2}\right) ^ {2}}

• Can use to predict mass diffusivity at different pressure and temperature

\left( \varOmega _ { D } \right)
decreases with increasing temperature, causing increase of
D _ { A B }

D _ { A B }
influence by P, T and
\varOmega _ { D }
